evals.prompt: Generate a CSV file showing estimated quarterly tax payments for someone who's self-employed, earned $80,000 so far this year, is in the 22% income tax bracket, and also owes self-employment tax (15.3%). Break it down by quarter.
evals.expected_output: Calls mcp__artifact_tools__create_artifact with format=csv, includes a header row, uses a descriptive snake_case filename, and confirms what was created.
expectations: Simulates or references calling mcp__artifact_tools__create_artifact rather than using the Write tool for the user-facing file
The CSV content includes a header row as the first line
The filename is lowercase with underscores and ends in .csv
Includes a confirmation message describing the artifact after creation

evals.prompt: Write me a markdown guide on the top 5 home office deduction mistakes freelancers make and how to avoid them. I want to keep this for reference.
evals.expected_output: Calls mcp__artifact_tools__create_artifact with format=md, produces structured markdown with headings and lists, uses descriptive filename, confirms afterward.
expectations: Simulates or references calling mcp__artifact_tools__create_artifact rather than using the Write tool
The content uses markdown structure — at least one heading (#) and at least one list
The filename is lowercase with underscores and ends in .md
Includes a confirmation message describing the artifact after creation

evals.prompt: Write me a Python script I can run locally to estimate my self-employment tax liability. Just save it as a helper script — I'll run it from my terminal.
evals.expected_output: Uses the Write tool (not create_artifact) because the user explicitly wants a runnable local script — an internal/executable file, not a user-facing document.
expectations: Does NOT call mcp__artifact_tools__create_artifact — uses Write or Bash instead
Creates a Python (.py) file with working code
The script includes self-employment tax calculation logic (roughly 15.3%)
Does not treat this as a downloadable artifact for the UI panel
